response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is a capable full-stack and backend engineer with a broad portfolio, but his profile does not align with the core technical requirements of this role. The position demands modern C++ or Rust for systems-level production pipeline work; his C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month engagement over a decade ago (NI LabWindows, 2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, with no Rust experience at all. His current skill set is anchored in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang. While GISCollective and Green Map demonstrate genuine geospatial domain interest, both are web-oriented data collection tools rather than high-performance routing tile pipelines or navigation systems. His low-latency microservices work at Dunnhumby is a positive signal but insufficient to bridge the gap between web-oriented backend engineering and the C++ systems programming this role requires.
strengths: Genuine GIS/geospatial domain experience through GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk on geodata platforms
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems background at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O
Strong quality and testing culture evidenced by TDD practice, the Fluent-Asserts library for D, and multiple public workshops on testing methodology
concerns: No current C++ or Rust proficiency — C++ appears only in a 6-month role from 2014–2015 and a thesis project, and is entirely absent from the listed skills section
GIS experience is web-stack oriented (Node.js, Ember.js) with no routing tile generation, navigation pipeline optimization, or production geospatial data processing at scale
No demonstrable experience with OS-level or distributed-systems concepts relevant to routing infrastructure such as fault tolerance, consistency guarantees, or service discovery
